ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic spaghetti food product class; no specific entry for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti with hotdog and banana ketchup sauce exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pasta dishes broadly; no specific code for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Filipino-style sweet spaghetti or Jolly Spaghetti as a specific clinical food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular serving of Jolly Spaghetti is approximately 250 g, as typically served at Jollibee fast food restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Jollibee Nutrition Information — Jolly Spaghetti
nutrient_source.organization: Jollibee Foods Corporation (official nutrition disclosure) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pasta with meat sauce entries for per-100g normalisation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Jollibee Philippines publishes per-serving nutrition data for Jolly Spaghetti. Per-100g values were derived by dividing published per-serving values by the standard serving weight of approximately 250 g. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 'Spaghetti with meat sauce' (NDB 27116) was used as a secondary cross-check for macro plausibility. The Jollibee Singapore menu at Bugis Village serves the same Jolly Spaghetti product. Values represent the regular (non-combo) serving.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — spaghetti, white, boiled
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jolly Spaghetti (Filipino-style sweet spaghetti). GI of 49 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for white spaghetti, boiled (GI ~49, low category), as the primary carbohydrate vehicle is boiled white spaghetti noodles. The sweet tomato-banana ketchup sauce adds simple sugars which may modestly elevate the effective GI; however, the pasta matrix dominates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (49 × 48.0) / 100 = 23.5. Carbohydrate per serving = 19.2 g/100g × 250 g = 48.0 g.

hypertension.guidance: A single 250 g serving of Jolly Spaghetti provides approximately 950 mg of sodium, representing nearly 41% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,300 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or avoid regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load and added sugar from sweet sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 23.5 per serving and approximately 16 g of added sugar from the banana ketchup-based sweet sauce, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Jolly Spaghetti in reduced portions and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, sodium, and processed meat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Jolly Spaghetti contains processed hotdog slices and a cheese topping contributing saturated fat (~3.5 g per serving) and high sodium (~950 mg per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose lower-sodium meal alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jolly Spaghetti is a signature fast food pasta dish sold exclusively at Jollibee restaurants, originating from the Philippines and now available at Jollibee outlets worldwide including Singapore. It consists of boiled white spaghetti noodles topped with a distinctively sweet, tomato-based sauce made with banana ketchup, ground pork, and sliced hotdog pieces, finished with a generous topping of grated cheese. Unlike Italian-style spaghetti bolognese, Jolly Spaghetti is characterised by its noticeably sweet flavour profile, which is a hallmark of Filipino-style spaghetti and is particularly popular with children. The sauce has a bright red-orange colour, a slightly thick consistency, and a sweet-savoury taste that sets it apart from Western pasta sauces. Nutritionally, a regular 250 g serving provides approximately 320 kcal, 12 g protein, 9 g fat, and 48 g carbohydrate, with a high sodium content of around 950 mg per serving. The dish has a low-to-moderate glycemic index (~49) driven by the pasta matrix, but the added sugar from the sweet sauce raises the glycemic load to approximately 23.5 per serving. Clinically, the high sodium content is a concern for individuals with hypertension, while the added sugar and moderate glycemic load require portion control for those managing Type 2 Diabetes. The presence of processed meat (hotdog) and saturated fat also warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease. The dish is listed as Halal at Jollibee Singapore. It is commonly served as a standalone meal or as part of a combo meal with fried chicken.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee spaghetti?

confidence_reason: Per-serving nutrition data is derived from Jollibee Philippines official nutrition disclosures and normalised to per-100g. The Singapore Jollibee menu serves the same product formulation. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy spaghetti with meat sauce was used as a secondary cross-check. GI is an analogous estimate based on white spaghetti from the University of Sydney GI Database, as no direct GI measurement for Jolly Spaghetti exists in the peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium rather than high because the per-100g values are derived rather than directly published.
notes: Jolly Spaghetti (D) in the input filename likely denotes a 'D' variant (possibly a dinner or drink combo designation on field survey); the canonical dish name is Jolly Spaghetti and nutritional values represent the standard regular serving. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure. The cuisine tag 'Halal' from the input is preserved in dietary_flags.is_halal=true. This food is served at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore, as recorded in the field survey source data.
